description_long: This object is a wooden electric fan with three elongated, slightly curved blades radiating from a central cylindrical hub. The blades are smoothly tapered and have a natural wood grain texture, giving the fan a rustic and handcrafted appearance. There is no visible protective blade guard, exposing the blades fully around the hub. The fan lacks any visible control panel, switches, or power cord, suggesting a minimalist or decorative design. The overall construction appears lightweight and simple, with all components made from the same wood material. The finish is matte, highlighting the organic texture and color of the wood.
description: A wooden electric fan with three exposed, smoothly tapered blades and a central cylindrical hub, featuring a natural wood grain finish and no visible controls or guard.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the fan from a slightly elevated angle, clearly displaying all three wooden blades and the cylindrical hub.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a similar perspective to view 0, with the blades' curvature and the wood grain texture visible.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a comparable angle, emphasizing the symmetry and tapering of the blades around the central hub.
description_view_3: View 3 maintains the same orientation, highlighting the exposed nature of the blades and the smooth, matte wood finish.
attribute_blade_guard_design: There is no blade guard present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective structure.
